findings.description: Page 2 table occupies a much longer vertical span in HTML, altering the compact slide-style layout.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the schedule table fits within a single slide-like page area beneath the title. In the HTML, the same table is stretched vertically with larger row heights, creating a long document-like layout and changing whitespace grouping.

findings.description: Page 2 footnote/link formatting is less prominent and visually separated differently from the table.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the footnote URL appears in large yellow text directly below the table and is highly visible. In the HTML, it appears as small plain text beneath the table, reducing emphasis though the content remains present.

findings.description: Table structure and merged-cell layout from the PDF were not faithfully preserved; content was duplicated into cells where the PDF visually uses a single merged region, changing row/cell mapping.
findings.evidence: In the PDF table, the 'Cape Fear – Neuse River Basin Hydrologic Model' column shows a single merged block labeled 'EMC Model Approval' spanning multiple dates (beginning around 1/1/2013 through 5/1/2013). In the HTML, this text is repeated in separate rows for 1/1/2013, 2/1/2013, 4/1/2013, and 5/1/2013. Similarly, on 11/14/2013 and 9/11/2014 the PDF shows entries only under 'Jordan Lake Water Supply Allocations', while the HTML duplicates those same entries into the 'Cape Fear River Basin Water Supply Plan' column.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy is not faithful to document structure: both slide titles are marked as h1, resulting in multiple top-level headings in a two-page slide deck rather than a single document title with subordinate headings.
findings.evidence:
HTML uses '

Agenda

' for page 1 and '

Jordan Lake Water Supply Storage Allocation - Updated Schedule

' for page 2. In the PDF, these are separate slide/page titles, not a document title plus section title hierarchy.

findings.description: Content is duplicated in the HTML table where it should not be, due to incorrect handling of merged cells.
findings.evidence: HTML repeats 'EMC Model Approval' across four rows and repeats 'Final Water Supply Plan and DWR's Allocation Recommendations presented at the EMC's November 2013 meeting.' and 'EMC decision at the September 2014 meeting.' in both the Jordan Lake and Water Supply Plan columns. In the PDF, these are not repeated text entries across those columns/rows.
